Space layout: per typical floor: 8 compact units along double-loaded corridor + core
Description: A high-rise apartment tower located in Shenzhen, China. The building footprint is approximately 38.7 m (east-west) by 25.8 m (north-south), rectangular, with the long axis rotated 225 degrees from north. Total gross floor area is about 14000.0 m^2 spread over 14 floor(s), giving roughly 1000.0 m^2 per floor. Floor-to-floor height is 3.1 m. The building is divided into 168 thermal zones in total (about 12 per floor). There are 6 dwelling units per floor (2 thermal zone(s) per unit), so model each unit as its own zone(s) stacked vertically across the 14 floor(s), plus a shared core/circulation. Typical space layout: per typical floor: 8 compact units along double-loaded corridor + core. Window-to-wall ratio is approximately 30% on exterior walls, with windows distributed on all four facades. Model all zones with appropriate constructions, glazing, occupancy, lighting, equipment and HVAC (ideal loads) so the IDF runs in EnergyPlus without errors.
